title: Download or export the machine-os image
body:
### Feature request description

For air-gapped Podman installations on Windows/macOS that don't require Podman Desktop, users can create a Podman machine with `podman machine init --image <machine-os.tar> ...`. Currently, if users want to download this image from quay.io, they have to use tools like `crane` / `skopeo`. Previously, these images were published in the GitHub releases of the respective projects, but this practice has [stopped](https://github.com/containers/podman-machine-wsl-os/issues/15) since a few months ago.



### Suggest potential solution

It would be helpful for users if there was a Podman command (e.g., `podman machine os download`) that would download and verify the latest machine OS image from quay.io for a platform of the user's choice, so that they don't have to manually do it on their own for different distros. This is what `podman machine init` does internally after all, we just have to expose it.

### Have you considered any alternatives?

The best alternative right now is to use third-party tooling (`crane` / `skopeo`).

### Additional context

Note that the obvious solution (`podman pull quay.io/podman/machine-os`) works only for macOS, but not for Windows. I think that's because the `machine-os-wsl` images uploaded to quay.io are not typical container images, and this trips Podman:

```
$ podman pull --arch x86_64 quay.io/podman/machine-os-wsl:5.4@sha256:06bb54537643e30d3ff4711e1556e1cef2e0fcb68221c1cd571c1e1018d5c8c3
Trying to pull quay.io/podman/machine-os-wsl@sha256:06bb54537643e30d3ff4711e1556e1cef2e0fcb68221c1cd571c1e1018d5c8c3...
Error: parsing image configuration: unsupported image-specific operation on artifact with type "application/vnd.oci.empty.v1+json"
```

I have some extra notes from my experimentation here: https://github.com/freedomofpress/dangerzone/issues/1145#issuecomment-2935272159

comments.body:
I rather not directly expose this on the podman binary, sure the code may exists internally but adding a command means documenting it and committing to support that forever.

For now WSL is special as it uses a rather old build process but that is about to change very soon as I will merge it into https://github.com/containers/podman-machine-os and that repo always publishes the changes to both quay.io and as normal files on the releases there https://github.com/containers/podman-machine-os/releases/tag/v5.5.0. That should make downloading the image you need trivial.

Oh, that's great news, a GitHub download link would solve this issue. If I may ask, is there an issue I can track for the WSL image migration to https://github.com/containers/podman-machine-os, so I can close this one?
Not on github but there is https://issues.redhat.com/browse/RUN-2491
Thanks, works for me. Closing this issue and will track the Red Hat one.

Ok, this should be all implemented starting with podman v5.6 in August.
github.com/containers/podman-machine-os/pull/142
Awesome, thanks for the heads up.
